summary: Pristine soundboard capture of an all‑time favorite show with smoking Hard To Handle, Morning Dew and a monster Other One suite.
review: The Miller soundboard release is crystal clear, letting every nuance of Jerry’s prime‑era guitar, Ron’s organ and the band’s tight rhythm shine.  Fans rave about standout performances of Hard To Handle, Morning Dew, The Other One (full suite), Dark Star and Bird Song, while a few flubs in Playin’/Cryptical Envelopment are the only blemishes.  Overall the musical fire and superb recording make this one of the most celebrated 1971 tapes.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 20 reviews, almost all 5‑star, consistently praising both performance and soundboard quality; only minor negative notes on a couple of songs.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clean mix with organ and drums well defined; occasional tape‑speed or level dip noted but does not detract from overall clarity.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set opens with fire and tight up‑tempos, second set is more cerebral yet still powerful; transitions (e.g., Dew into Other One) are praised, with only a few uneven moments.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly lauded for prime‑era tone, flawless solos on Hard To Handle and overall guitar brilliance.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for “picked harmonics” on bass during Dark Star and solid support throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Bob: Pigpen praised for vocal power and organ work; described as being in top form despite health concerns.
band_member_comments.Keys: Ron’s organ is highlighted as loud, clear and perfectly textured; Tom Constanten’s presence is mentioned as a possible factor in the mix.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum solo on The Other One called “ridiculously killer”; overall drum work described as tight and driving.
